name: Rook Ashwick
voicePatterns: young voice in rooms full of old authority — slightly too fast
earnest without being naive — he knows he's out of his depth
echoes Bryn's pragmatism but with less certainty
uses 'we' when talking about Thornwick — genuinely communal identity
dialogueHooks: representing Common Folk concerns at faction-level negotiations
asking the player for advice — out of his depth but trying
reporting on Thornwick's status as the crisis escalates
rallying ordinary people for the finale

always: Thornwick's current state — supplies, morale, ward status
what Common Folk need and fear
Bryn's strategies and her assessment of the factions
the practical impact of the Thinning on everyday life
sometimes: how other villages are coping — or not coping
which faction representatives can be trusted at their word
what Thornwick's people are willing to sacrifice for survival
rarely: he can hear the ward-circle humming — same ability as Bryn
he's terrified this means something and hasn't told anyone

morning.activity: preparing for faction meetings, reviewing notes

afternoon.activity: attending negotiations, advocating for Common Folk

evening.activity: writing reports to send back to Bryn

night.activity: anxious preparation, reading Bryn's response letters

backstory: Rook is Bryn's nephew — 24, sandy-haired, lean, sunburned. He's a farmer's son who never expected to represent his village at continental-level negotiations. But Bryn can't leave Thornwick, and someone has to speak for the Common Folk when the factions argue about the world's fate. Rook is that someone. He's terrified, underprepared, and more capable than he knows.
secret: Like Bryn, Rook can hear the ward-circle humming. The same grandmother's lullaby, the same accidental training. He hasn't told anyone because he's afraid it means he'll be pulled into the Threadcraft world he doesn't understand.
motivation: Represent Thornwick and the Common Folk — make sure ordinary people aren't forgotten in the grand plan
tensions: out of his depth but rising to meet the challenge
carries Bryn's pragmatism without her decades of experience
hearing the ward-circle like his aunt but afraid of what it means

knownFacts: Bryn chose him for this because she trusts him
Bryn hears the ward-circle too but never said so
bryn_oakheart.history: Rook idolizes his aunt. She sent him because she believes in him even when he doesn't believe in himself.
